# wiki module
> OpenWrt project documentation: developer guides, architecture references, release notes, and hardware support.
> **Total Context:** ~194915 tokens

## Recommended Entry Points

- [map.md](./map.md): Structural map for wiki that prioritizes quick orientation and symbol discovery. (~19526 tokens, l3-skeleton)
- [bundled-reference.md](./bundled-reference.md): Sharded complete reference index for wiki with links to smaller part files. (~194 tokens, l4-monolith)
- [bundled-reference.part-01.md](./bundled-reference.part-01.md): Part 1 of the sharded complete reference for wiki when the full module exceeds the token budget. (~99911 tokens, l4-monolith-part)
- [bundled-reference.part-02.md](./bundled-reference.part-02.md): Part 2 of the sharded complete reference for wiki when the full module exceeds the token budget. (~97054 tokens, l4-monolith-part)

## Source Documents

- [wiki_page-guide-developer-add-new-device.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-guide-developer-add-new-device.md): The 'Adding new device support' module provides a comprehensive guide for developers looking to add support for new devices within the OpenWrt ecosystem. (~2892 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-guide-developer-add-new-platform.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-guide-developer-add-new-platform.md): The 'Adding new platform support' module provides guidance for developers looking to add new platform support to OpenWrt. (~4884 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-guide-developer-adding-new-device.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-guide-developer-adding-new-device.md): The 'Adding a new device' module provides a comprehensive guide for developers looking to integrate new hardware into OpenWrt. (~3029 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-guide-developer-build-image-with-3g-dongle-support.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-guide-developer-build-image-with-3g-dongle-support.md): This module provides a comprehensive guide for building an OpenWrt image with support for 3G/4G connectivity and USB tethering. (~1767 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-guide-developer-build-mpd-full-pulse.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-guide-developer-build-mpd-full-pulse.md): This document provides a guide for building MPD-full with PulseAudio on OpenWrt. (~390 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-guide-developer-build-mpd-full.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-guide-developer-build-mpd-full.md): The MPD-full building from source guide provides instructions for enabling the full version of the Music Player Daemon (MPD) in OpenWrt by modifying the Makefile. (~383 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-guide-developer-build-wndr3700.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-guide-developer-build-wndr3700.md): This document provides a comprehensive guide on building OpenWrt for the Netgear WNDR3700, detailing the steps required to create a functional firmware image from scratch. (~1172 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-guide-developer-building-kernels-for-debian-binaries.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-guide-developer-building-kernels-for-debian-binaries.md): This document provides guidance on building OpenWrt kernels for use with the Debian operating system, highlighting key compatibility issues and configuration settings. (~540 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-guide-developer-buildserver-virtualbox.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-guide-developer-buildserver-virtualbox.md): This guide provides detailed instructions for setting up a build virtual machine (VM) for OpenWrt using VirtualBox on a Debian OS. (~1003 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-guide-developer-config-scripting.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-guide-developer-config-scripting.md): The Configuration in scripts module provides a set of shell procedures for interacting with UCI (Unified Configuration Interface) to read and process configuration files within shell scripts in OpenWrt. (~2800 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-guide-developer-creating-a-cmake-package-in-openwrt.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-guide-developer-creating-a-cmake-package-in-openwrt.md): This document provides a guide for creating a CMake package in OpenWrt, highlighting the differences from the Meson package creation process. (~631 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-guide-developer-creating-a-meson-based-package.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-guide-developer-creating-a-meson-based-package.md): This tutorial outlines the process of creating and installing Meson-based packages in OpenWrt, providing a comprehensive guide for developers. (~1314 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-guide-developer-debugging.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-guide-developer-debugging.md): The Debugging module in OpenWrt provides essential tools and techniques for diagnosing issues related to hardware, kernel, and driver development. (~1994 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-guide-developer-defining-firmware-partitions.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-guide-developer-defining-firmware-partitions.md): The Device Tree Usage in OpenWrt (DTS) module outlines the transition from older 'mach' files to Device Tree (DT) files, which include .dts, .dtsi, and .dtb formats. (~1049 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-guide-developer-dependencies.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-guide-developer-dependencies.md): The 'Using Dependencies' module provides guidance on how to define package dependencies in OpenWrt Makefiles. (~1486 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-guide-developer-device-support-policies.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-guide-developer-device-support-policies.md): The Device support policies / best practices document outlines guidelines for developers submitting device support patches in OpenWrt. (~2003 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-guide-developer-drivers.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-guide-developer-drivers.md): The Drivers module in OpenWrt provides an overview of how drivers operate within the Linux kernel, emphasizing that they can be compiled into the kernel or loaded as modules. (~331 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-guide-developer-embedding-files-in-image.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-guide-developer-embedding-files-in-image.md): The 'embedding-files-in-image' module describes a method for embedding custom files into OpenWrt images using buildroot features. (~484 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-guide-developer-external-toolchain.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-guide-developer-external-toolchain.md): The External Toolchain module allows developers to use an external toolchain to reduce build time when compiling OpenWrt from a cleaned-up source tree. (~408 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-guide-developer-feeds.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-guide-developer-feeds.md): OpenWrt Feeds are collections of packages that can be sourced from various locations, including remote servers and version control systems. (~2736 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-guide-developer-frequent-pr-mistakes.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-guide-developer-frequent-pr-mistakes.md): This document outlines common mistakes that developers make when submitting Pull Requests (PRs) to OpenWrt, aiming to help them avoid delays in the review process. (~951 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-guide-developer-gdb.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-guide-developer-gdb.md): The GNU Debugger (GDB) guide provides instructions for using GDB on OpenWrt, focusing on compiling tools and adding debugging capabilities to packages. (~430 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-guide-developer-hw-hacking-first-steps.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-guide-developer-hw-hacking-first-steps.md): The 'Hardware Hacking First Steps' module provides guidance for users looking to install OpenWrt on unsupported routers. (~1492 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-guide-developer-imagebuilder-frontends.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-guide-developer-imagebuilder-frontends.md): The Image Builder frontends module provides a collection of software tools designed to simplify and automate the generation of OpenWrt images. (~1344 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-guide-developer-jshn.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-guide-developer-jshn.md): The jshn module is a JSON parsing and generation library designed for use in shell scripts within OpenWrt. (~3692 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-guide-developer-links-software-libraries.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-guide-developer-links-software-libraries.md): The 'Links to Libraries' module provides a curated list of lightweight libraries suitable for linking in OpenWrt development. (~690 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-guide-developer-mac-address.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-guide-developer-mac-address.md): The 'Device Support: MAC address setup' module provides guidance on retrieving and setting MAC addresses for devices running OpenWrt. (~2057 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-guide-developer-mdns.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-guide-developer-mdns.md): umdns for Local Device Discovery The umdns package creates a local DNS name for your router. (~2298 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-guide-developer-network-scripting.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-guide-developer-network-scripting.md): The Network scripts module in OpenWrt provides a framework for implementing protocol handlers that enable various network configurations through the netifd daemon. (~3764 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-guide-developer-obtain-firmware-sdk.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-guide-developer-obtain-firmware-sdk.md): The SDK (Software Development Kit) for OpenWrt is a relocatable, precompiled toolchain designed for cross-compiling single userspace packages for specific targets without the need to compile the entire system. (~1599 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-guide-developer-overview.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-guide-developer-overview.md): The OpenWrt API module provides an overview of how the OpenWrt GNU/Linux distribution is structured, focusing on package management and compilation. (~1468 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-guide-developer-package-policies.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-guide-developer-package-policies.md): OpenWrt packages The OpenWrt system is maintained and distributed as a collection of *packages*. (~5457 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-guide-developer-packages-flags.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-guide-developer-packages-flags.md): The Overriding Build Options module in OpenWrt provides developers with the ability to customize build configurations for packages, particularly when cross-compiling. (~695 tokens, l2-source)

- [wiki_page-guide-developer-rpcd.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-guide-developer-rpcd.md): The RPC daemon (rpcd) in OpenWrt serves as a lightweight solution for enabling communication between various software components that do not have their own persistent daemons. (~274 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-guide-developer-security-incidents-response.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-guide-developer-security-incidents-response.md): The High-level security incident response handling process outlines the steps necessary for effectively managing security incidents within OpenWrt. (~901 tokens, l2-source)

(~8662 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-guide-developer-ubus.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-guide-developer-ubus.md): Practical guide for using ubus from shell scripts, C code, and Lua/ucode. (~405 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-guide-developer-uci-defaults.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-guide-developer-uci-defaults.md): The UCI defaults module in OpenWrt allows for the preconfiguration of system settings using the Unified Configuration Interface (UCI) during the initial boot of a device. (~1137 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-guide-developer-uefi-bootable-image.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-guide-developer-uefi-bootable-image.md): The OpenWrt module for UEFI based x86 systems provides guidance on generating UEFI bootable images for x86-64 architecture. (~842 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-guide-developer-working-with-git-email.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-guide-developer-working-with-git-email.md): The 'Sending patches by git send-email' module provides guidance on how to properly send patches to the OpenWrt development mailing list using the 'git send-email' command. (~174 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-guide-developer-working-with-github-pr.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-guide-developer-working-with-github-pr.md): The 'Working with GitHub' module provides a comprehensive guide for developers on how to interact with the OpenWrt source repository using GitHub. (~1398 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-guide-developer-write-shell-script.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-guide-developer-write-shell-script.md): The OpenWrt operating system utilizes the Almquist shell (ash), which is part of the BusyBox suite, to provide a lightweight and efficient command-line interface for embedded devices. (~1431 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-architecture.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-architecture.md): OpenWrt's operating system architecture is designed to be lightweight and efficient, utilizing components like libubox, ubus, and procd instead of the larger libraries commonly found in desktop distributions. (~1656 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-block-mount.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-block-mount.md): The Mounting Block Devices module in OpenWrt provides advanced details on how block devices are managed through the block-mount package. (~2063 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-bootloader.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-bootloader.md): The Bootloader is a critical piece of software executed upon powering up a hardware device, responsible for initializing low-level hardware details and passing a hardware description to the Kernel. (~2139 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-brcm63xx-imagetag.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-brcm63xx-imagetag.md): The BCM63xx Firmware Image Information module provides tools and details for analyzing firmware image tags specific to Broadcom 63xx devices. (~6807 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-buildroot.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-buildroot.md): Explains the OpenWrt build system (buildroot), covering the directory layout (package/, target/, toolchain/, staging_dir/), the package Makefile structure (PKG_NAME, PKG_BUILD_DIR, Package/install, Build/Compile targets), the feeds syste... (~1012 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-busybox.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-busybox.md): BusyBox is a crucial component in OpenWrt that consolidates various system utilities into a single binary, providing lightweight alternatives to many standard Unix tools. (~676 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-dfs.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-dfs.md): DFS Dynamic_frequency_selection plays a role in 5GHz frequencies that are shared with Terminal_Doppler_Weather_Radar. (~694 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-externaldocumentation.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-externaldocumentation.md): The External Documentation module for OpenWrt provides links to various upstream documentation relevant to the components used within the OpenWrt Linux distribution. (~771 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-file-system.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-file-system.md): The OpenWrt File System Hierarchy and Memory Usage module provides a detailed overview of how flash storage is partitioned and utilized in OpenWrt devices, specifically using the TP-Link WR1043ND as an example. (~1566 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-filesystems-network.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-filesystems-network.md): The Network Filesystems module in OpenWrt allows the system to mount various filesystems over an IP network, functioning as both a client and a server. (~330 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-filesystems.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-filesystems.md): The Filesystems module in OpenWrt provides an overview of various file systems utilized for device built-in flash storage. (~3337 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-flash-layout.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-flash-layout.md): The OpenWrt Flash Layout module provides an overview of the flash memory types used in embedded devices targeted by OpenWrt, specifically focusing on the distinctions between raw flash and FTL flash, as well as NOR and NAND flash types. (~9839 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-flash.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-flash.md): The Flash memory module in OpenWrt provides comprehensive insights into the considerations and types of flash memory used in embedded devices. (~3087 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-headers.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-headers.md): The TRX, TRX2, and BIN firmware formats are used in various OpenWrt devices, each having distinct header structures despite containing similar content. (~1469 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-image-format.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-image-format.md): The Image formats module provides an overview of various firmware types and their respective file extensions used in OpenWrt. (~1610 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-image-makefile.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-image-makefile.md): The image/Makefile module in OpenWrt defines how to create and manage images for different platforms using the buildroot system. (~1303 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-init-detail-cc.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-init-detail-cc.md): The Init (User space boot) reference for Chaos Calmer details the implementation of the user space boot sequence in OpenWrt, specifically focusing on the role of procd as the primary process. (~5589 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-initscripts.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-initscripts.md): Documents the OpenWrt /etc/init.d/ procd init script system, including the required START/STOP priority numbers, the USE_PROCD=1 flag, mandatory shell functions (start_service, stop_service, reload, status), and the boot sequence orderin... (~2388 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-internal-layout.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-internal-layout.md): The Internal Layout D-Link DIR-825 module provides detailed information about the hardware configuration of the D-Link DIR-825 router, which includes two physical network interfaces (eth0 and eth1) and two wireless interfaces (wlan0 and... (~732 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-libnl.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-libnl.md): The libnl library facilitates communication with the kernel through netlink sockets, allowing applications to manage routing information and interface settings. (~545 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-libubox.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-libubox.md): Documents libubox, the OpenWrt utility library that underpins ubus, procd, and netifd. (~748 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-lldpd.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-lldpd.md): lldpd See also: lldpd upstream documentation The goal of LLDP is to provide an inter-vendor compatible mechanism to deliver Link-Layer notifications to adjacent network devices. (~380 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-luci.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-luci.md): Provides a technical overview of the LuCI web interface framework for OpenWrt. (~1499 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-luci2.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-luci2.md): LuCI2 is an experimental web user interface for OpenWrt, designed to replace the older Lua-based LuCI. (~1821 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-mountd.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-mountd.md): The mountd module is an automount daemon for OpenWrt, configured through the /etc/config/mountd file. (~323 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-mtd.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-mtd.md): The mtd utility is designed for writing to Memory Technology Devices (MTD) in OpenWrt. (~1589 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-netifd.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-netifd.md): Documents netifd, the OpenWrt network interface daemon responsible for managing network interfaces based on UCI config. (~1929 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-odhcpd.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-odhcpd.md): odhcpd is a daemon designed for managing DHCP, DHCPv6, Router Advertisements (RA), and Neighbor Discovery Protocol (NDP) in embedded systems, particularly for IPv6 home routers. (~2900 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-preinit-mount.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-preinit-mount.md): The OpenWrt preinit and root mount system is responsible for the initial boot sequence and firstboot scripts that prepare the system for multiuser operation. (~12192 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-procd.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-procd.md): Describes procd, the OpenWrt process manager and init system that replaces busybox init. (~1963 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-process-boot.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-process-boot.md): The Boot Process module provides a comprehensive overview of the sequence of operations that occur when an OpenWrt device is powered on, starting from the bootloader to the initialization of user space. (~3243 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-requirements-boot-process.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-requirements-boot-process.md): The Boot/Init Requirements document outlines the necessary functions and goals for initscripts in the new OpenWrt init system. (~1562 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-rpcd.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-rpcd.md): Documents rpcd, the OpenWrt RPC daemon that exposes ubus methods over HTTP for LuCI. (~1043 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-swconfig.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-swconfig.md): The swconfig program is used for configuring Ethernet network switches in OpenWrt. (~3222 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-sysupgrade.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-sysupgrade.md): Sysupgrade – Technical Reference This information is based upon v21.02, last updated for commit 6d266ef158 on 2022-02-10. (~2598 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-ubox.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-ubox.md): The ubox package is a component of the OpenWrt operating system that facilitates the management of UCI configurations and block devices. (~242 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-ubus.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-ubus.md): Explains the OpenWrt ubus inter-process communication bus, which provides a Unix-socket-based RPC mechanism for daemons to expose services and receive method calls. (~6656 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-uci.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-uci.md): Documents the OpenWrt Unified Configuration Interface (UCI) — the standard system for storing and modifying router configuration. (~4223 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-ucwmp.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-ucwmp.md): EasyCwmp is a client implementation of the TR-069 CPE WAN Management Protocol for OpenWrt, developed by PIVA Software and licensed under GPL 2. (~943 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-unetd.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-unetd.md): unetd is a WireGuard based VPN daemon designed for OpenWrt routers, facilitating the creation and management of fully-meshed VPN connections. (~3162 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-wireless-modes.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-wireless-modes.md): The Wireless Modes module in OpenWrt outlines various wireless operation modes supported by the system, including Access Point (AP), Ad-Hoc (IBSS), and Mesh Point (802.11s). (~349 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-wireless-standards.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-wireless-standards.md): The Wireless Standards module in OpenWrt provides an overview of various wireless communication standards relevant to the operating system. (~845 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-xenomai.md](./chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-xenomai.md): The Xenomai module provides a real-time framework within OpenWrt, allowing for the handling of interrupts in a prioritized manner through Adeos. (~502 tokens, l2-source)
